title: Gelatin and Thickeners
content:
GELATIN

Unflavored gelatin sets approximately 2 cups liquid per tablespoon ("bloom" strength 150). High sugar concentrations retard setting. For clear gelatins: 1 tablespoon to 2 cups liquid gives firm mold after 2 hours chilling. For softer texture: 1 tablespoon to 2½-3 cups liquid (serve in cups, not unmolded).

Mixing Methods:
1. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon gelatin over ¼ cup cold water; soak 3 minutes without stirring. Add to 1¾-2 cups boiling liquid; stir to dissolve.
2. For cold liquid: Soften gelatin in ¼ cup cold water; dissolve over hot water; add to 1¾-2 cups 75°F liquid.
3. Quick-set: Soften as above; use 1 cup boiling fruit juice/stock; stir in 8-10 ice cubes 2-3 minutes; remove unmelted ice; let stand 3-5 minutes; incorporate solids; mold.

Agar (seaweed): 1 teaspoon per cup liquid. Soften in ¼ cup cold liquid; dissolve in ¾ cup hot.

Irish Moss/Carrageenan: 6-10 grams powder per cup liquid. Heat to 140°F to dissolve.

BREAD CRUMBS

Dry: Made from dry bread/zwieback/cake. Grind when cool; measure as sugar. Store cool, dry, loosely covered.

Soft: Use 2-4 day-old bread. Pull apart with fork; do not crush. Pile lightly into cup; do not pack unless soaking and squeezing.

Buttered/Browned: For each cup dry crumbs, brown slowly in ⅓ cup butter with ½ teaspoon salt. Add chopped bacon, nuts, cheese, or paprika as desired.

Croutons: Dice bread; saute in butter or olive oil until even brown; or butter slices, dice, and brown in 375°F oven. Season with salt, paprika, Parmesan, or herbs while hot.

Breading/Crumbing Foods: Dry food first if moist surface. Season flour or crumbs. For bound breading (à l'anglaise): flour, dip in beaten egg (2-3 teaspoons water or milk per egg), then seasoned dry crumbs. Dry 20 minutes before frying. Do not refrigerate breaded foods before frying.

Au Gratin: Light coating of dry or fresh bread crumbs (or crushed cornflakes/crackers/nuts) on scalloped/sauced dishes. Brown under broiler or in oven. For cheese au gratin, add grated cheese and dots of butter to crumbs.

FLOURING, BREADING, CRUMBING

Food should be 70°F and dry. For even coating, put seasoned coating and food in paper/plastic bag and shake. For fragile foods, use standard breading procedure.
